dialogue: Hi, Father. I'm Veda. I'm new to the area and was curious about your church. Could you tell me a little bit about it?

Of course, Veda. Our church is based on the teachings of Jesus Christ. We believe in loving our neighbor and treating others as we would want to be treated. We strive to live according to Christ's example and teachings.
That sounds like a really great way to live. I can see why so many people are drawn to this religion. What do you think makes Christianity different from other religions?

Well, there are a lot of different interpretations of Christianity, but for us, it's all about following Jesus Christ's example. He was a man who loved unconditionally and forgave easily. He preached compassion and understanding, even for those who disagreed with him or did not follow his teachings perfectly. We try to emulate that in our own lives."
That does sound different. I know that forgiveness is a big part of Christianity, but it's not always easy to do. How do you think Christians are able to forgive so easily?

Well, I think it comes from our belief that everyone is capable of change and redemption. We believe that no one is beyond hope or help, and so we are always willing to forgive those who have wronged us. It's not always easy, but it is something we strive for.
narrative: Veda thought about going to church because she was interested in the religion. She had never been to church before, but she had heard good things about it. She decided to go to a nearby church and see what it was like.

dialogue: Hey Shavon, what's up? You seem troubled.
Yeah, I am. I'm just having a hard time and needed someone to talk to.
Of course, man. I'm always here for you. What's going on?
It's just everything. Work is stressing me out, my relationship is falling apart, and I feel like I'm losing touch with my friends. I don't know what to do.
Well, let's start with work then. What's going on there?
It's just that everything is so demanding and I can't keep up. I'm constantly behind and it feels like I'm never going to catch up.

Okay, that does sound pretty tough. But it sounds like you're doing the best you can under the circumstances. Maybe you just need to cut yourself some slack and focus on one thing at a time. As for your relationship, what's going on there?
We're just fighting all the time and it feels like we're growing apart instead of together. I don't know if we can make it through this rough patch.
Do you still love her?
Yes, I do. But it's just so hard right now.
Then you should definitely try to work things out. It sounds like you're going through a lot right now, but your friends are still here for you. So don't forget that.
narrative: Rylea sat down with Shavon and asked him what was wrong. Shavon told Rylea that he was having a hard time and needed someone to talk to. Rylea said that he would be happy to be there for his friend.

dialogue: Hi there! Are you looking for someone to take care of your pet while you're out of town?
Yes, I am. I'm going on vacation for a week and I need someone to watch my cat.
That's no problem at all! I love taking care of animals, and I have experience with cats. I'll be happy to take care of your cat while you're gone.
That sounds great. How much do you charge?
I charge $20 per day, plus an additional $10 per day if you want me to stay overnight.
That sounds reasonable. I'll definitely need you to stay overnight, so it will be $30 per day total. When can you start?
Any time that works for you! Just let me know when you'll be leaving and when you'll be back, and I'll take care of everything in between.
Alright, I'll be leaving on Saturday and I'll be back next Saturday. Can you come over on Friday evening to meet my cat and get everything set up?
Yes, that sounds perfect. I'll see you on Friday at 6pm.
narrative: Jabriel provides a service by taking care of people's pets while they are out of town. Jabriel is a helpful person who loves animals, so she decided to start this business. She has been taking care of pets for three years and has never had any problems.

dialogue: Hey, Mattea. What's up?
Not much, what's up with you?
Just hanging out, same old same old. So, what's new with you?
Well, I've been thinking about what you said the other day.
What did I say?
You know, about how I should stand up to my dad and stop letting him control my life.
Oh yeah, that.
Yeah, I've been thinking about it a lot and I've decided that you're right. I'm going to start standing up for myself more and not let him control me anymore.
That's great, Mattea! I'm really proud of you for making that decision.
Thanks, Danna. It's not going to be easy, but I'm determined to do it.
You can do it, Mattea. I know you can.
narrative: Danna always shares Mattea's point of view because he is loyal to his friend. Danna never questions Mattea's decisions and always stands by his side, no matter what the situation is.

dialogue: I know what you're thinking, Dontrell. I can sense your feelings and know exactly what you're going to say or do next. You're thinking about how much you miss your family, and how you wish you could see them again. You're wondering if there's anything you can do to make that happen. And you're also feeling a little guilty, because you know that if you could see them again, it would only be for a short while before they died.
Cornell, how can you possibly know all that? It's like you can read my mind!

I told you, I can sense your feelings. It's like I have a connection to your thoughts and emotions. I'm not sure why or how it works, but ever since we met, I've been able to understand what you're thinking and feeling pretty easily.
Wow...that's amazing! And a little bit scary too. But it does explain why we've always been so close - even though we've only known each other for a few months.
Yeah, I guess our connection is pretty strong. But don't worry, Dontrell - I'll never use it to manipulate or control you in any way. I promise."
That's a relief! I was starting to feel a little bit like you could read my mind, and it was kind of creepy. But if you promise not to use your abilities to control me, then I'm okay with it.
I swear, Dontrell. I'll never use my abilities to control or manipulate you in any way. You have my word.
narrative: Cornell knows what Dontrell is thinking. He can sense her feelings and know exactly what she is going to say or do next.

dialogue: Hi, God. I'm feeling a little anxious about my math test tomorrow. I'm just not sure if I'm going to be able to do it. Can you give me some guidance?
Of course, Bram. I will always be here for you. Just remember that I am with you and that I will help you through anything. You can do this, Bram. Just have faith and believe in yourself.
Thank you, God. Just knowing that you're with me makes me feel a lot better. I'll try my best and I know that with your help, I can do it.
That's the spirit, Bram! I know you can do it.
narrative: Bram was worried about his upcoming math test. He prayed to God for help and guidance. After praying, Bram felt more confident and comforted.

dialogue: You're asking for a lot, Tinsley. We've been partners for a long time and I think you know that we're not in a position to give you what you're asking for.
What exactly are you asking for?
I want a larger percentage of the profits and a say in how the product is marketed.
Why do you think you deserve that?

Because I'm the one who came up with the product in the first place. It's my name on it and I should be getting a bigger cut of the profits. And as for marketing, I know what works and what doesn't. I can help make sure that our product is presented in the best light possible.
Hmm...I see your point. Let me talk to the other partners and see what they think. I'll get back to you soon.
narrative: This time, Tinsley wants a larger percentage of the profits and a say in how the product is marketed.

dialogue: I'm so glad you're home! I have a surprise for you.
What is it?
Come inside and see!
Okay!
Wow! This is amazing! Thank you so much, Rachelle!
You're welcome. I wanted to make your birthday extra special this year.
narrative: Rachelle had a plan to make Alysson's birthday special. She secretly invited all of Alysson's friends over and decorated the house with balloons and streamers. When Alysson came home from school, she was surprised and happy to see all her friends there waiting for her.

dialogue: Hi, Pietro. How are you doing today?
I'm doing well, Aariz. Thanks for speaking with me.
Of course! So, what's been going on?
Well, I've just been really busy lately. Work has been keeping me busy and I haven't had much time for anything else.
That sounds tough. I know how it feels to be too busy to do anything else but work. It's not fun.
Yeah, it's not fun at all. But I'm trying to make the best of it.
That's good! I think that's the best we can do in situations like these. Just try to keep our heads up and power through it.
Yeah, that's what I'm trying to do. Thanks for the encouragement, Aariz
narrative: Aariz spoke kindly to Pietro, and he felt his day brighten. Aariz was pleased that she could make Pietro's day better.

dialogue: So, can you tell me a little bit about the job?
Well, it's a customer service position. You'll be working with our clients, helping them with whatever they need.
I see. And what are the qualifications for this job?
Well, we're looking for someone who is friendly and helpful, and who has experience dealing with customers.
I definitely have those qualifications. I've been working in customer service for years, and I love helping people. I'm confident that I would be great at this job.
Great! We'll give you a call if we decide to move forward with your application.
narrative: Makennah had the perfect qualifications for the job and he also had the right attitude. Makennah was very determined to get this job and he did everything possible to make sure that he would be the right person for it. In the end, Makennah got exactly what he wanted: The Job.

dialogue: I find it amusing that the events of the world are so far removed from my own life.
What do you mean?
Well, take this news story for example. It's about a conflict in some country that I've never even heard of. What does it have to do with me?
I'm not sure. What do you think it has to do with you?
Probably nothing. But it's just interesting to think about how much is happening in the world that I have no connection to whatsoever.
I guess that's true. But even if you're not directly affected by something, it can still be important to know about it.
Why?

Well, for one thing, it helps you understand the world better. And also, if something bad happens in another country, it could have an indirect effect on you. For example, if there's a war going on somewhere, that could cause the price of oil to go up, and that would affect everyone.
Hmm, I see your point. I hadn't thought of it like that.
So even though these events may seem far removed from your life, they can still have an impact on you in ways that you might not expect.
narrative: Arizona was sitting in his armchair, listening to the news on the radio. He found it amusing that the events of the world were so far removed from his own life.

dialogue: Hey, thanks for taking my motorcycle off my hands. I really appreciate it.
No problem, man. It's a nice bike. I'm sure I'll enjoy riding it.
I'm just glad to be rid of it, to be honest. It was costing me a lot in repairs and maintenance.
Yeah, motorcycles can be pretty expensive to keep up. But they're also a lot of fun to ride. So it's worth it, in my opinion.
I'm not so sure. I think I'd rather just take the bus from now on. It's cheaper and less hassle.
That's a valid point. But sometimes it's nice to have the freedom that a motorcycle provides. You can go where you want, when you want.
I guess that's true. But it's just not worth the expense for me anymore.
Well, I'm glad you sold it to me. I'll take good care of it.
narrative: Tatianna sells his motorcycle to a man in a parking lot. He feels relieved because he no longer has to worry about the cost of repairs and maintenance.

dialogue: "I'm really glad I could help you out, Avarie. I know how much your grandmother means to you."
"Yeah, she's been sick for a while now and I've been meaning to visit her. But with work and everything else, it's been hard to find the time."
"I know how that is. It can be tough to balance everything."
"Yeah. But I'm glad I was finally able to make the trip. And thank you again for driving me to the airport and helping me with everything."
"Of course, no problem at all. I'm just happy I could help."
"Well, I should probably head through security. Thanks again, Aryn."
"Yeah, no problem. Take care of yourself, Avarie."
"I will. And give my best to your grandmother."
narrative: Aryn makes Avarie's trip because Aryn wants to help Avarie visit his sick grandmother. Aryn drives Avarie to the airport, helps him check in, and sees him off at security.

dialogue: I'm sorry, Daddy.
Why did you let the dog go?
He was whimpering and I felt bad.
Now we'll never find him.
I'm sorry.
Yitzchok, you have to be more careful. We can't just let animals go because we feel bad for them.
I know, Daddy. I'm sorry.
narrative: Yitzchok felt sorry as soon as she let the dog go. She had been holding onto the leash tightly, but the dog's whimpering had finally convinced her to let go. As soon as the dog was free, it ran away.

dialogue: Hi, can I ask you a question?
Of course! What would you like to know?
How long does it usually take for someone to become fluent in a new language?

Well, that really depends on the person. Some people pick up new languages very quickly, while others take a bit longer. There are a lot of factors that can affect how fast someone learns a new language, such as motivation, intelligence, and prior knowledge.
Hmm, that's interesting. Do you think there's anything I can do to help me learn faster?

Definitely! There are lots of things you can do to help yourself learn more quickly. For example, studying regularly is important. Try to set aside some time each day to review what you've learned. You might also want to try using flashcards or other memory aids to help remember vocabulary words. Additionally, listening to native speakers and practicing speaking as much as possible will also be beneficial.
narrative: Tressa was interested in learning. She started making progress by studying for a few hours each day. Within a month, she could speak fluently and was able to understand most of what was said to her.

dialogue: Thank you so much for all your help in selling my house. I really appreciate it.
You're welcome, Deandra. It was my pleasure to help you out. I'm just glad that everything went smoothly and that the house sold quickly.
Yeah, me too. I'm really excited about the move, but it's a bit bittersweet leaving this place behind. It's been my home for so long.

I know how you feel. It can be tough to let go of a place that holds so many memories. But sometimes change can be a good thing, and it sounds like this new opportunity is going to be great for you. Plus, you never know - maybe you'll end up coming back to this city someday and buying another house here! Stranger things have happened. ;)
Ha, yeah. I guess you're right. Change can be good. Thanks for everything, again.
You're welcome. Good luck with everything!
narrative: Deandra's house was sold because Deandra is moving to a new city for work. Deandra's realtor did a great job and the house sold quickly.

dialogue: Sarah, I love the outdoors! I love hiking and camping, and spending time in nature. It's just so beautiful and peaceful out there.
Yeah, I know what you mean. There's just something about being in nature that feels so good for the soul.
I know! I feel so connected to the world when I'm outdoors. It's like everything just makes sense.
Yeah, it's like you can finally breathe and relax.
Exactly! I feel so lucky that we live in a place where we have access to all of this beautiful nature.
Yes, it definitely makes living here worth it.
narrative: Salah loves the outdoors because it gives her a chance to appreciate the natural world. She loves hiking and camping, and enjoys spending time in nature whenever she can.

dialogue: Hi, I'm Tyrique.
Hi, I'm Lily. Are you flying to London today?
Yes, I am. I'm excited to be visiting the city for the first time.
It's a great city. I'm sure you'll love it. Have you been before?
No, this will be my first time. What about you? Do you live there?
Yeah, I moved there about a year ago for work and absolutely love it. There's always something new to do and see.
That sounds amazing! I can't wait to explore everything that London has to offer. Have any recommendations of where I should start?
Definitely! There's so much to do that it can be overwhelming, but if you're looking for some iconic sights, I would recommend starting with Buckingham Palace, Big Ben, and Westminster Abbey.
Wow, those all sound like places I definitely need to see. Do you have any other recommendations?
For sure! If you're interested in art, the British Museum and the National Gallery are both must-sees. And if you want to get a feel for London's history, make sure to check out the Tower of London.
That sounds like a lot to fit into one trip! I'm going to have to come back again sometime.
Absolutely. London is a city that always has something new to offer.
narrative: Tyrique gets on a plane at JFK airport. She is flying to London Heathrow. The flight is eight hours long.

dialogue: Hi, Mr. Saunders. I was just wondering if I could ask you a question.
Sure, Curtis. What's on your mind?
I was just wondering... do you have any plans to retire soon? Or sell the bakery?
Oh, I hadn't really thought about it. To be honest, I'm not sure what I would do without this place. It's been my life for so long.
I see. Well, I was just wondering because I would be interested in buying the bakery from you when you're ready to retire.
Oh, Curtis. I don't know what to say. That's a very generous offer. Let me think about it and get back to you, okay?
Okay, Mr. Saunders. Thanks for considering it.
narrative: Curtis has worked at the bakery for four years. He has learned every aspect of the business and believes he could run it himself. Curtis has been saving money and plans to purchase the bakery from the current owner in two years.

dialogue: I'm not going back to that school. I hate it.
Why do you keep getting kicked out?
I don't know. I just don't like it there, that's all. And all the kids are phony.
What's a phony?
You know, someone who's not real, who puts on an act. All the kids at my school are phonies. They're all trying to be something they're not. It's all a big act.
But what are you trying to be?
I don't know...I'm just myself, I guess. But everyone else is always putting on an act and it drives me crazy.
You're not going back to school and you don't know what you want to do with your life. Is that it?
I guess so. Yeah, pretty much.
Well, what are you going to do?
I don't know. Maybe just travel around or something. See the country.
narrative: Naydelin develops the reputation of being a rebel. He gets into fights and is constantly getting kicked out of school. He doesn't care what people think of him and he wants to be seen as an adult.

dialogue: I'm telling you, man, she is just too good for me. I don't deserve her.
Dude, stop being so negative. You're a great guy and she clearly loves you.
I don't know...I just feel like I'm not good enough for her. She's so amazing and I'm just...I don't know.
Sade, you are an amazing guy and she loves you. Just relax and enjoy it.
I guess you're right. I'm just being too hard on myself.
Exactly. Now go enjoy your amazing girlfriend. You deserve it!
narrative: Sade was having a conversation with his friend. He felt proud of himself because he was able to hold an intelligent conversation.

dialogue: I can't believe it's still raining.
I know, dear. It's been raining for days now.
Yeah, I know. I just wish it would stop already.
Well, I don't think it will be stopping anytime soon.
Ugh, I really don't like the rain.
Yes, it can be quite unpleasant can't it?
narrative: Calum finds that the rain did not go. Now Calum gets wet as she steps outside and feels the cold droplets fall on her head and run down her face.

dialogue: I can't believe I agreed to do this. I'm already so behind on everything else.
What's wrong? You seem really stressed out.
I am. I feel like I can never catch up. Every time I turn around, there's something else that needs to be done. And it's all because I keep taking on too much.
Why do you keep doing that?
I don't know. I guess I just feel like if I don't, then I'm not really doing my part or contributing enough. But it's just too much sometimes and it makes me so stressed out.
Well, maybe you need to start saying no more often. Just because someone asks you to do something doesn't mean you have to say yes. You have a right to say no and set your own limits.

Yeah, maybe you're right...I should start saying no more often. It's just so hard though when people are counting on me and asking for favors all the time. But maybe if I start setting some boundaries, it'll help reduce my stress levels in the long run. Thanks for talking with me about this, Lily - it really helped put things into perspective!
narrative: Yamir is a high school student who often takes on too much work. She frequently signs up for more extracurricular activities than she can handle and regularly agrees to do favors for her friends, even when she's already overwhelmed with other commitments. As a result, Yamir is constantly stressed out and feels like she can never catch up.

dialogue: Hi, I was just checking my account balance and it's missing $200. Can you tell me what happened?
Let me take a look. When did you last check your balance?
Yesterday.
And how much was in your account then?

Okay, let me check to see if there have been any recent transactions. (After a few moments) There was a withdrawal of $200 from an ATM on Main Street yesterday afternoon. Do you remember using that ATM?
No, I didn't use it yesterday. Someone must have stolen my card and used it without my knowledge. Is there any way to get my money back?
We can refund the money to you, but it will take a few days for the transaction to be reversed. In the meantime, we can cancel your card and issue you a new one.
That would be great, thank you.
No problem, we'll take care of it right away.
narrative: Adiel discovers the loss when she goes to check her bank account balance and sees that the $200 is missing. She immediately contacts her bank to see if they can refund the money.

dialogue: Maria, I'm feeling really sick. I have a high fever and a rash all over my body. I'm so weak and tired, all I want to do is just rest.
Oh no, Emmaleigh! You have the measles! You need to go to the doctor right away!
I don't think that's necessary. I'll just drink lots of fluids and rest, and I'll be fine in no time.
Emmaleigh, you could get very sick from the measles. It's best to err on the side of caution and see a doctor just to be sure. Plus, they can give you medication to help make you more comfortable.
Maria, I appreciate your concern, but I really don't think seeing a doctor is necessary. I'll be fine, I promise.
Well, if you're sure that's what you want to do, then I'll support you. But please just take care of yourself and rest as much as possible.
narrative: Emmaleigh has the measles and is feeling very sick. He has a high fever and a rash all over his body. He is weak and tired, and all he wants to do is rest.

dialogue: Hey True, how are you today?
I'm doing well, Rahim. How about you?
I'm good. So, what have you been up to lately?
Not much. Just hanging out with friends and family. You know, the usual stuff.
Yeah, I know what you mean. So, do you have any plans for the future?

Well, I'm still in school right now so I'm just focusing on that for the time being. After I graduate though, I'd like to travel a bit and see different parts of the world. There's so much to see and experience out there and I just want to make the most of it while I can.
That sounds amazing! I've always wanted to travel myself but haven't had the opportunity yet. Hopefully one day we can both travel together and check things off our bucket lists!
narrative: Rahim talks to True because Rahim is interested in his life and enjoys his company. True makes her laugh and feel comfortable, so she wants to be around him more.

dialogue: I'm really glad you gave me the opportunity to lead the team in your absence. It's something I've been wanting to do for a while now.
Well, you certainly proved yourself today. You did a great job leading the team and handling everything that came up. I'm impressed.
Thank you, I appreciate that. I really wanted to show you that I'm capable of leading and managing the team effectively.
You definitely did that today. You showed excellent leadership skills and made sure everything ran smoothly. I'll be happy to recommend you for future leadership opportunities.
narrative: Jazz gets a chance to prove herself when she is asked to lead the team in the absence of the manager. Jazz wants to prove that she is capable of leading the team and show her skills. She successfully leads the team and proves herself to be a competent leader.

dialogue: I just feel so trapped, man. I can't even begin to tell you how unhappy I am right now.
Yeah, I can imagine. It's been tough for you lately.
It feels like everything is just falling apart and there's nothing I can do about it. I'm just stuck here, living off my parents and barely scraping by.
Have you looked into getting a job?
Yeah, I've been applying to places but it's been months and I haven't had any luck. And even if I did get a job, it wouldn't be enough to get me out of this hole I'm in.
Well, what about going back to school? You could get a degree and then look for a better paying job.
Yeah, but that would take years and I don't know if I can wait that long. Plus, I don't even know if that would guarantee me a good job after all that time and money spent on school.
Have you considered moving out of your parents' place?
Yeah, I've thought about it but I can't afford it on my own. And even if I could find a roommate, I don't know if I could handle living with someone else right now.
Well, what do you want to do then? Just stay here and be unhappy for the rest of your life?
No, of course not. But it feels like no matter what I do, I'm just stuck in this rut and I don't know how to get out of it.
Vicki, you're not going to be stuck in this forever. Things will get better, you just have to be patient and keep trying. Everything will work out in the end, trust me.
narrative: Vicki is unhappy because he feels trapped. He has been unemployed for months and has been struggling to make ends meet. He's been forced to move back in with his parents and feels like he's never going to be able to get his life back on track.

dialogue: Wow, I can't believe I built this all by myself! It looks amazing.
You did a great job, Mahnoor. This building is really impressive.
Thanks, Sarah. I'm pretty proud of it. It took a lot of hard work, but it was worth it.
I can imagine. How long did it take you to build it?
Oh, about a month. I worked on it every day after school and on weekends.
That's a really long time. I don't think I could've been that patient.
Yeah, it was tough at times. But like I said, it was worth it in the end.
narrative: Mahnoor constructs a building. The building she creates is tall and imposing, with lots of windows to let in natural light. It's clear that Mahnoor is proud of her accomplishment; she stands back to admire her handiwork with a satisfied smile on her face.

dialogue: I just feel so stuck in my job. I've been doing the same thing for years and I just can't seem to move up or get out.

Yeah, I can definitely understand how you feel. It's tough when it feels like you're stuck in a rut. But sometimes, it can be helpful to look at your situation from a different perspective. Maybe there are things you could be doing differently in your job that would help you to move up or get out?

I don't know, I've tried everything I can think of. And even if there were things I could be doing differently, I don't know if it would make any difference. It just feels like no matter what I do, I'm not going to get anywhere.

Well, it's worth considering anyway. And even if it doesn't work out the way you want it to, at least you tried something different and made an effort to change your situation. That's always better than feeling stuck and hopeless. So why don't you give it some thought and see if anything comes to mind? Who knows, maybe something will come up that you hadn't considered before that could really help you out!
Yeah, I guess you're right. It's worth a shot. Thanks for the talk, it really helped me to see things in a different light.
No problem, anytime!
narrative: Kenzie was talking to his best friend about how he felt stuck in his job. His best friend listened patiently and then offered some advice that helped Kenzie see his situation in a new light. Now Kenzie feels motivated to make a change.

dialogue: Hey, Sarah. What's up?
Hey! Not much, what about you?
Same here. Just getting ready for school. I don't really like having to think about what to wear every day, so I just stick to the same outfit.
Yeah, I can understand that. I'm always trying to find new outfits and it's just a lot of work. Plus, it's hard to find something that looks good and is also comfortable.

Exactly! I don't want to have to worry about whether my clothes are going to be uncomfortable or if they're going to look good. I just want to be able to put on the same thing every day and know that it will work.
Yeah, that would be nice. But sometimes it's fun to mix things up and try new styles. Have you ever tried wearing something different?

No, not really. I mean, I'll switch out my shoes sometimes or wear a different color shirt, but nothing too drastic. I like knowing what I'm going to wear each day. It makes getting dressed in the morning a lot easier.
I can see that. But it must get kind of boring wearing the same thing all the time.
Not really. I don't really care what other people think about my clothes. I'm comfortable with what I'm wearing and that's all that matters to me.
narrative: Tenley is a high school student who likes to be consistent. She always wears the same clothes to school so that she doesn't have to worry about what to wear in the morning. Tenley's favorite outfit is a black t-shirt and jeans with her Converse sneakers.

dialogue: Hey, Sasha. How are you doing?
I'm doing alright. Just been a little stressed lately. School is just really tough right now.
Yeah, I can imagine. But you're so smart, I'm sure you'll get through it no problem. You always seem to find a way to succeed no matter what the challenge is.
Thanks, Anastazia. That means a lot coming from you. You're just always so confident and everything seems to come so easily to you. I wish I could be more like that.

To be honest, it's not all rainbows and butterflies like it may seem from the outside looking in. I have my own struggles and insecurities too, we all do. It's just a matter of not letting them get the best of us and continuing to push forward no matter what life throws our way
You're right. We all have our own battles to fight. But it's good to know that even someone like you who seems to have it all together still has to deal with the same issues as everyone else.
Exactly. No one is perfect. We're all just human beings doing the best we can with what we have.
narrative: Anastazia was born into a wealthy family and has always had everything he could ever want. He is smart, good-looking, and athletic, and he's never had to work a day in his life. Things just seem to always go his way.

dialogue: I can't believe I lost the winning ticket. I'm so angry right now.
I'm sorry to hear that, Mariely. What can I do to help you?
There is nothing you can do. I had the winning ticket and now it's gone.
Well, let's see if we can retrace your steps and see if we can find it. When did you last have it?
The last time I remember having it was when I bought it yesterday morning.
Okay, so we'll start there. Do you remember where you bought it?
Yes, at the grocery store on Main Street.
Great, we'll go there first then. Maybe someone turned it in or found it there.
I doubt it, but it's worth a try I guess.
Absolutely. Even if we don't find the ticket, at least we tried.
narrative: Mariely was the lucky winner of the lottery, but when he went to collect his prize, he realized he had lost the winning ticket. He was devastated and angry, especially since he knew that there was no way to replace it.

dialogue: Hey, Pedro. What's up?
Not much. Just working on some new invention ideas. What are you up to?
I'm working on a new invention too. I'm trying to create a device that would allow people to communicate with each other without speaking.
That sounds really interesting! I'd love to hear more about it.
Well, I'm still in the early stages of development, but I'm hoping that it will be able to send and receive messages using a form of telepathy.
That's amazing! I can't wait to see it when it's finished.
narrative: Abilene was working on a new invention. He was trying to create a device that would allow people to communicate with each other without speaking.

dialogue: I'm almost done. I just have to dry the dishes and put them away.
Okay, sounds good. Let me know when you're finished so I can check everything over.
Will do!
Hey mom, I'm finished with the dishes.
Let me take a look.
Okay.
Everything looks good! You did a great job.
narrative: Brittanie knows that in order to wash the dishes correctly, he must fill up the sink with hot soapy water. He then proceeds to wash each dish individually, making sure to scrub them clean before rinsing them off in the hot water. Once all of the dishes are clean, Brittanie dries them off with a towel and puts them away in their respective cabinets.

dialogue: Mom, I know my clothes are muddy, but I had so much fun! Playing in the mud is just so much fun.
Asante, you're going to have to change out of those clothes before you come back inside. You're just going to make a mess if you come inside like that.
No, Asante, your whole outfit is muddy. You need to take everything off and put on something clean.
But Mom! This was so much fun! Can't I just play in the mud for a little longer? Please?
No, Asante. You've been playing in the mud long enough. It's time to come inside and get cleaned up now.
narrative: Asante was outside playing in the mud, and she got her clothes dirty. She didn't care that she was getting muddy, and she had a lot of fun.

dialogue: I can't believe I've been waiting in this line for so long. It feels like forever.
It's only been five minutes. Relax, it'll be your turn soon.
Yeah, I know. But it feels like forever.
I understand how you feel. Time can seem to move very slowly when we're waiting for something, especially if we're not sure how long it's going to take.
narrative: Teara was frustrated because she had been waiting in line for a long time. She looked at her watch and saw that she had only been waiting for five minutes, but it felt like forever.

dialogue: Why are you crying? What's wrong?
I'm just really upset right now. I can't believe you would yell at me over something so small.
I'm sorry, I didn't mean to make you cry. I was just really angry in the moment. But I shouldn't have taken it out on you like that. Can we please talk about this calmly?
I don't know, Casper. I just feel really hurt right now.
Erik, I'm sorry. Please, can we talk about this? I don't want to fight with you.
Okay, let's talk. But I'm still feeling really upset.
Yeah, I understand. But like I said, I'm sorry for what happened. It won't happen again, I promise.
narrative: Casper and Erik's first fight was over something small, but it quickly escalated. Casper started yelling and Erik started to cry. The fight ended with both of them feeling angry and upset.

dialogue: Hey, thanks for the ride.
No problem. I'm just glad I could help out.
Yeah, I'm sure your parents would be happy to know you're helping me out.
They're always happy when I help people out. It makes them feel good knowing that I'm a good person.
That's great. My parents are always happy when I help people out too. It makes them feel like they raised me right.
That's really great. I'm sure they're proud of you.
narrative: Natanael pulled up to the train station and Ashleigh jumped out of the car. "Thanks for the ride," Ashleigh said as he slammed the door shut. Natanael nodded and watched as Ashleigh ran into the station.

dialogue: Hey, Rose. I was thinking about changing my hair back to its original color.
Really? Why?
I don't know. I just feel like people are making fun of me and I don't like it.
Who's making fun of you?
Everyone. They say I look like a clown with this red hair.
Oh, Saphire. I think you're being too sensitive. Your hair looks fine.
You really think so?
Of course! You could never look like a clown, honey.
narrative: Saphire is ridiculed in the comments section for his new hairstyle. People say he looks like a clown, and that his hair is too red. Saphire is unhappy and decides to change his hair back to its original color.

dialogue: Hi there! How are you today?
I'm doing well, thank you. How are you?
I'm doing great, thanks for asking. So what brings you in today?
I need some new tires for my car.
Okay, we can definitely help you with that. What kind of car do you have?
A Toyota Camry.
We have a few different options for tires that will fit your Camry. Let me show you what we have and then we can figure out which one is the best option for you.
narrative: Usher is a very friendly man and he always makes sure his customers are happy. He always has a smile on his face, and he loves to talk to people. His customers always leave his store happy and satisfied.

dialogue: I can't believe you're the best basketball player in our class.
Yeah, I guess I am.
I've been practicing so hard to be the best, and you just beat me out of nowhere.
Sorry, Gabi. I didn't mean to make you feel bad.
It's not your fault. I'm just really competitive and I want to be the best at everything we do.
Well, maybe we can have a friendly competition sometime and see who comes out on top.
Yeah, that sounds like fun. Thanks, Caylie.
narrative: Layth has been trying to beat Caylie at everything they do. Recently, Layth found out that Caylie is the best basketball player in their class. Layth has been practicing hard to be the best basketball player in their class.

dialogue: Wow, Laisha. You look absolutely stunning. I can't help but admire your beautiful body.
Thank you, Agatha. I must say, you look pretty good yourself.
I can't help but notice how firm and perky your breasts are. They look absolutely amazing.
Thank you! I've been working hard to keep them looking their best.
Your efforts have definitely paid off! Can I ask, what's your secret?
I honestly don't know. I just try to take care of myself as best as I can.
Well, whatever you're doing, it's working. You have an amazing body.
Thank you! I appreciate the compliment.
Your skin looks so smooth and creamy. It's like porcelain.
Thank you! I moisturize every day and night to keep it looking its best.
It's definitely working! Your skin looks incredible.
Thank you!
And your ass...I mean, wow! It's so firm and round. It looks amazing in those jeans.
Thanks! I do a lot of squats to keep it looking its best.
Whatever you're doing, keep doing it because it's working wonders! Your butt is absolutely gorgeous.
narrative: Agatha walks in on Laisha bathing and becomes entranced by the sight. She can't help but notice Laisha's creamy skin, pert breasts, and firm ass. Agatha begins to feel a stirring in her loins and decides that she needs to bathe with Laisha.

dialogue: Hey, Sarah! I'm so glad you're here. I've been dying to show you my new dance routine.
That sounds great! I can't wait to see it.
Okay, let me just go change into my dancing clothes. I'll be right back!
Sure, no problem.
What do you think? Isn't it amazing?
It's definitely impressive! You've obviously been practicing a lot.
Yeah, I have. I really wanted to make sure that it was perfect before showing anyone.
Well, it definitely paid off! You looked like a professional dancer out there.
Thank you so much, Sarah! That means a lot coming from you.
narrative: Tidus was an attention-seeker. She loved being the center of attention and always showed off her latest achievements. Tidus was always seeking approval and validation from others.

dialogue: I just feel like I'm not good enough, you know? No matter what I do, it's never enough.
It sounds like you have some pretty negative thoughts about yourself. Can you tell me more about that?

Yeah. I guess I just feel like I'm always falling short, no matter what. Like, I'll work really hard at something and then it turns out bad or someone else does it better than me and it just feels awful.
That doesn't sound like a very positive way to view yourself or your accomplishments. What do you think might be causing these negative thoughts?

I don't know. Maybe it's just how things have always been for me. Whenever something goes wrong, it feels like it's all my fault somehow. And even when things are going well, I just don't feel good enough somehow.
That doesn't sound like a very healthy way to live your life. Are you willing to try and change that?
Yes, I think so. It's just really hard sometimes to see myself in a positive light when everything around me seems so negative. But I'm willing to try if it will help me feel better about myself.
Well, let's start by exploring some of the reasons why you might feel this way. Do you think there are certain situations or events that trigger these negative thoughts?
Yeah, I guess so. Like I said, whenever something goes wrong or I don't meet my own expectations, it really bothers me.
So it sounds like you're pretty hard on yourself. Can you think of any other times when you might feel this way?
Yeah, I guess any time I'm compared to others or have to compete with someone. It just makes me feel really inferior and not good enough.
That definitely sounds like a lot of pressure that you're putting on yourself. Would you be willing to try and let go of some of that pressure? Maybe give yourself a break every once in awhile?
Yeah, I think that would help. Just knowing that I don't have to be perfect all the time would take a lot of the stress off.
That's definitely a good idea. Why don't we try some relaxation techniques to help you let go of some of that tension and stress?
narrative: Shan seeks counseling to help address his low self-esteem. In counseling, Shan learns ways to cope with his negative thoughts and begins to see himself in a more positive light. He starts to feel more hopeful about his future and takes pride in his accomplishments.

dialogue: Man, I'm starving.
Yeah, me too. There's nothing to eat around here.
I found a half-eaten sandwich in one of the trashcans.
That's gross! You're not going to eat that, are you?
I don't know. I'm so hungry, I might have to.
Well, if you're going to eat it, at least make sure it's clean first. You don't want to get sick from eating garbage.
narrative: Zayne scoured the area for any signs of food. He rummaged through the trashcans and found a half-eaten sandwich. Zayne was happy to have found something to eat.

dialogue: Hey Alayla, what's up?
Not much, what's up with you?
I'm just feeling really frustrated with how things are going on the soccer team. I feel like I'm not being used to my full potential and it's really frustrating me.

Yeah, I can understand that. It's tough when you feel like you're not being given a chance to show what you can do. But you have to remember that the coach is making the decisions about who plays and who doesn't based on what he thinks is best for the team. Sometimes that means leaving players on the bench who deserve to be playing. It's not personal, it's just business.

I know that, but it still doesn't make it any easier to deal with. I feel like I'm good enough to be starting every game, but instead I'm stuck riding the bench. It feels like a waste of my time and talent.

I know how you feel, but you have to trust that the coach knows what he's doing. He's been doing this a lot longer than you have and he has the team's best interests at heart. Just keep working hard in practice and be ready to take your opportunity when it comes.
I guess you're right. Thanks for talking to me, man.
No problem, anytime.
narrative: Luna is an aggressive player on the soccer team. He is always getting into arguments with the other players and the coach. Alayla, the captain of the team, has had to talk to him numerous times about his behavior.

dialogue: I'm really glad to be out of the hospital and back home. It was tough being in there, but I'm feeling a lot better now.
I'm glad to hear that Charla. How are you doing with your medication? Are you still taking it as prescribed?
Yes, I am. And I'm feeling a lot better because of it. My moods are more stable and I'm not having any hallucinations or anything like that anymore. So I guess the medication is working well for me.

That's great to hear! It sounds like you're really making progress with your treatment. But we need to keep an eye on things and make sure that you continue taking your medication as prescribed and attending therapy sessions regularly. If you start to feel like your symptoms are coming back or worsening, please don't hesitate to reach out to us so we can adjust your treatment plan accordingly.
Okay, I will. Thanks for all your help, doctor.
You're welcome, Charla. We'll be here to support you every step of the way.
narrative: Charla was released from the psychiatric facility because he no longer met the criteria for involuntary commitment. He had been receiving treatment for bipolar disorder and had been stabilized on medication for several months. His doctor and therapist agreed that he could continue treatment as an outpatient and posed no threat to himself or others.

dialogue: Hey, Sarah.
Hey, Justina. What's up?
Not much. Just hanging out with friends.
Yeah, same here. I'm glad we get to spend some time together. We've been so busy lately.
Yeah, I know. But it's good to see you nonetheless. So what have you been up to?
Just the usual, school and hanging out with friends. You know how it is.
Yeah, I do. But it's always good to catch up. So how's school been?
It's been good. I'm getting straight A's again this semester.
That's great! I knew you could do it.
Thanks, Justina. I couldn't have done it without your support.
Of course, anything for my best friend. So what are your plans for after graduation?
I'm not sure yet. I've been thinking about going to college, but I'm not sure what I want to study yet.
Yeah, that's a tough decision. But you'll figure it out eventually. You're smart and you'll make the right choice."
narrative: Justina spends time with friends. Now Justina gets closer to his best friend, Sarah. Justina and Sarah have been close since they met in kindergarten, and now they spend almost every day together.

dialogue: Hey, coach. Great game today.
You played well out there, Delilah. You were really hustling.
Yeah, I wanted to make sure we got the win.
That's the attitude I like to see. You're a leader out there on the court. Keep it up and you'll go far in this game.
narrative: Delilah played an important role in the game because he wanted to be a leader. He worked hard to control the ball and score some points. His teammates were inspired by his determined attitude and they went on to win the game.
